context: hex-cli exposes 44+ top-level command groups. Many share responsibilities (status/pulse/brief, swarm/task, new/init/scaffold). New users see a wall of commands with no narrative. Error handling relies on .unwrap()/expect() in several primary commands, and output formatting is inconsistent (some commands emit JSON by default, others plain text, few support --json uniformly). This workplan addresses the top 5 highest-leverage improvements identified in an audit on 2026-04-13.
intent: Make hex feel like a cohesive AI Operating System, not a collection of 50 subcommands. Focus: discoverability, consistency, error clarity, and autonomy gaps that push users toward manual invocation.
out_of_scope: Shell completion regeneration (already handled by clap)
Command renames beyond group moves (too disruptive this cycle)

phases.rationale: hex status, hex pulse, hex brief, and the bare `hex` command all overlap. A new user cannot tell which to run. Each emits slightly different data with different formatting. This is the single biggest source of 'hex feels like 50 tools' confusion.

tasks.acceptance: Document overlapping vs unique fields; choose canonical home for each
tasks.title: Audit status/pulse/brief output — map fields to a single StatusSnapshot struct

tasks.acceptance: `hex status` shows one coherent dashboard with sections (health, agents, plans, inbox); `hex brief` is a thin alias; deprecation notice on brief
tasks.title: Promote `hex status` to be the canonical single-project view; `hex pulse` stays multi-project; `hex brief` becomes `hex status --brief` (alias kept)

tasks.acceptance: `hex status --json | jq` works; same for pulse/brief
tasks.title: Add --json to all three for machine consumers; default stays human-formatted with color+tables

phases.title: Unify status/pulse/brief into a single ambient surface

phases.rationale: Some commands have --json, some emit JSON by default, some have no machine-readable mode at all. Destructive commands vary in whether they prompt. Scripting hex is currently painful.

tasks.acceptance: Flags parsed at top level, propagated to subcommands via clap global=true
tasks.title: Define a GlobalFlags struct in hex-cli/src/cli.rs — --json, --quiet, --no-color, --yes

tasks.acceptance: OutputContext respects --json/--quiet/--no-color; human output uses consistent prefixes (ok:, warn:, err:, info:)
tasks.title: Thread flags through an OutputContext (stdout formatter) — replace scattered println!/eprintln! with ctx.info/warn/error

tasks.acceptance: No destructive command runs without confirmation in a TTY or --yes flag
tasks.title: Audit destructive commands (plan drafts clear, swarm cleanup, worktree cleanup, send-override, agent disconnect, trust set) — all require --yes or interactive confirm

phases.title: Global flag consistency — --json, --quiet, --no-color, --yes

phases.rationale: Panics in a user-facing CLI emit Rust backtraces, which look like hex is crashing. Each one should be a user-friendly anyhow::Context error.

tasks.acceptance: Full list with file:line + risk classification (user-triggerable vs genuinely-unreachable)
tasks.title: Grep hex-cli/src/commands/ for .unwrap()/.expect() — produce inventory

tasks.acceptance: Remaining .unwrap() calls are all on static regexes, compile-time constants, or documented-unreachable paths
tasks.title: Replace user-triggerable unwraps with .context("...")? and typed anyhow errors

tasks.acceptance: cargo run -p hex-cli -- <bad-args> shows human error, not backtrace
tasks.title: Add a top-level error handler in main.rs that formats anyhow chains as 'Error: X\n  caused by: Y' (no backtrace unless RUST_BACKTRACE=1)

phases.title: Eliminate .unwrap()/.expect() from primary command paths

phases.rationale: `hex --help` shows 44 top-level commands. Users cannot scan this. Most belong in the existing `config`, `dev`, or `override` groups which were partly rolled out but left incomplete. Finish the consolidation.

tasks.acceptance: `hex dev skill list` works; top-level `hex skill` kept as deprecated alias for 1 release
tasks.title: Move skill/assets/context/hook under `hex dev` (developer/platform tools)

tasks.acceptance: `hex brain taste set`, `hex brain neural-lab experiment` work
tasks.title: Move neural-lab, brain, taste under `hex brain` (all self-improvement/learning)

tasks.acceptance: `hex system doctor`, `hex system stdb health` work; legacy aliases kept
tasks.title: Move stdb, nexus, sandbox, doctor, self-update under `hex system` (runtime/infra)

tasks.acceptance: Every top-level group has example output in --help
tasks.title: Add `hex <group> --help` examples block — every group shows 2-3 concrete example invocations

phases.title: Command grouping cleanup — collapse 44 groups to ~20 via subgrouping

phases.rationale: hex doctor finds problems but requires the user to manually invoke the fix. `hex go` is the next-action command but doesn't triage broken environments. Ship `hex fix` as the 'turn the key and it starts' command for an AIOS.

tasks.acceptance: `hex fix --dry-run` lists what would be fixed; `hex fix` applies them
tasks.title: Create hex-cli/src/commands/fix.rs — enumerates remediable doctor failures

tasks.acceptance: Each remediator is idempotent, prints what it did, and verifies success
tasks.title: Wire remediators: nexus-not-running → `hex nexus start`, stdb-down → start spacetimedb, stale agents → `hex agent prune`, unsynced assets → `hex assets sync`, stale drafts → `hex plan drafts gc --days 7`

tasks.acceptance: If nexus is down when `hex go` runs, output suggests `hex fix` not 'connection refused'
tasks.title: Teach `hex go` to suggest `hex fix` when doctor checks fail, instead of raw error

phases.title: Autonomy gap: `hex fix` — auto-remediate common diagnostic failures

prerequisites: Existing autonomy features remain untouched: hex (no args), hex go, hex hey, hex brain daemon, hex plan reconcile
success_criteria: `hex --help` fits on one screen (<30 top-level entries)
Every command supports --json or is documented as human-only
Zero panics from user-triggerable input in hex-cli/src/commands/
`hex fix` recovers a broken environment in one command
`hex status` is the canonical project view; pulse/brief are clearly differentiated
title: hex CLI Polish — AIOS Ergonomics Pass
